summary: A rectangular, multi-story fort-like building with four tall corner towers connected by a three-story central block. The main block has horizontal stone slab bands and many small windows; towers are vertical dark wood columns with overhanging flat platform roofs. The complex sits on a shallow stone-slab perimeter with a raised central walkway/roof pattern visible from above.

shape.overall_form: central rectangular block with four corner towers connected by short walls/roofed walkways
shape.symmetry: roughly bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis; rotational symmetry not exact but similar corners

materials.name: fence
materials.role: short railing elements between towers (low confidence)

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging flat roofs
horizontal stone slab bands around central block
small square windows in repeating rows
central cross-shaped flat roof/walkway visible from above
rebuild_hints: Create a rectangular footprint with towers at each corner offset slightly inward from edges; towers are tall vertical logs with wood plank infill.
Make central block three levels tall with repeating rows of 1x1 windows separated by horizontal stone slab/stonebrick bands approximately every 4 blocks of height.
Top roofs are layered flat slabs (stone slabs) with a darker inner slab or block layer forming an inset cap on each tower and on small roof sections above the central block.
Place stone slab or stonebrick block pads along the ground around the perimeter (visible as single-block pads) to match the base detailing.
Add a central raised walkway/roof in a cross shape composed of stone slabs with darker accents at the cross arms as seen from top view.
uncertainties: Exact block types of the horizontal bands: appear as stone/stonebrick or slabs—could be slab_stone or stonebrick.
Whether the darker roof layer is nether brick, dark wood, or colored wool/concrete; top inset appears dark red/purple (uncertain).
Precise interior floor heights and internal layout are not visible.
Ground material around the building (reddish dirt) could be coarse dirt or terracotta; identified only by color.
